title: Daily Settlement Batch (SETTLE_BAT + RPT_DAILY)
description: Legacy Pro*C daily settlement batch system. Calculates interest, fees, and taxes for all accounts, updates balances, and generates daily reports. Written over 15 years by multiple developers with no refactoring.

source_files.description: Main settlement batch program. Processes all accounts: interest calculation, fee calculation, tax calculation, settlement posting, balance update.

functions.name: main
functions.description: Main entry point. 400+ lines of inline logic including arg parsing, DB connect, cursor loop, inline calculations, GOTO error handling, commit management.

functions.name: calc_interest
functions.description: Interest calculation with tiered rates, promotion rates, account-type-specific logic, period-based rates for fixed deposits.

functions.name: calc_fee
functions.description: Fee calculation with transaction-based fees, balance-based fees, maintenance fees, grade-based exemptions.

functions.name: proc_settlement
functions.description: Settlement posting with sequence generation, row locking (SELECT FOR UPDATE), retry logic, settlement history INSERT.

functions.name: print_summary
functions.description: Prints batch processing summary to log and stdout.

functions.name: sig_handler
functions.description: Signal handler for SIGINT/SIGTERM. Rollback and cleanup on abnormal termination.

source_files.description: Daily report generator. Produces fixed-width text reports: branch summary, customer grade stats, account type stats, limit-over report.

functions.name: main
functions.description: Entry point. DB connect, batch result check, calls report generators.

functions.name: gen_branch_report
functions.description: Branch-level settlement report using Oracle CONNECT BY for tree hierarchy.

functions.name: gen_grade_report
functions.description: Customer grade statistics using Oracle DECODE for pivot.

functions.name: gen_type_report
functions.description: Account type statistics with GROUP BY aggregation.

functions.name: gen_limit_report
functions.description: Limit-over accounts report with top-100 cutoff.

source_files.description: Shared header with error codes, macros, struct definitions, logging macros. Included by both .pc files.

sql_files.description: Oracle DDL for all 14 tables, indexes, sequences, comments, and reference data.

static_cursors: c_acct (SETTLE_BAT: main account cursor, ALL branches — 4-table join with outer joins)
c_acct_br (SETTLE_BAT: main account cursor, specific branch — duplicate of c_acct with branch filter)
c_br_rpt (RPT_DAILY: branch report with CONNECT BY PRIOR tree traversal)
c_type_rpt (RPT_DAILY: account type GROUP BY report)
c_limit_rpt (RPT_DAILY: limit-over accounts DESC by over amount)
select_into: TB_TXN_HIST COUNT/SUM aggregate with CASE WHEN
TB_PROMO_RATE promotion rate lookup with date range and ROWNUM=1
TB_FEE_MST fee rate lookup with 2-table join and date range
TB_ACCT_MST balance SELECT FOR UPDATE NOWAIT (in proc_settlement)
DECODE pivot query for grade stats (RPT_DAILY — 10 columns in single query)
insert: TB_SETTLE_DAILY (with UPSERT: INSERT then UPDATE on DUP_KEY)
update: TB_BATCH_CTL status to C or E (batch end)
transaction_control: ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT sp_acct (on ERR_HANDLE and SKIP_ACCT)
SELECT FOR UPDATE NOWAIT with retry loop (in proc_settlement)

oracle_specific: NVL() — null handling throughout all queries
DECODE() — pivot aggregation in grade report
ROWNUM = 1 — top-1 lookup for promo rate and covid exempt
CONNECT BY PRIOR ... START WITH — branch hierarchy tree
ORDER SIBLINGS BY — sibling ordering in tree traversal
outer join (+) syntax — Oracle proprietary outer join in main cursor
SELECT FOR UPDATE NOWAIT — pessimistic row locking
ORA-00054 handling — resource busy retry loop
sqlca.sqlcode == 1403 — no data found (end of cursor / empty result)
TO_CHAR / SUBSTR for date string manipulation in queries
goto_labels: ERR_HANDLE (SETTLE_BAT main: per-account error recovery, rollback savepoint, log, continue)
SKIP_ACCT (SETTLE_BAT main: skip current account, rollback savepoint, continue)
BATCH_END (SETTLE_BAT main: exit main loop, close cursor, final commit)
CLEANUP (SETTLE_BAT main: summary print, file close, DB disconnect)
DB_CONN_ERR (SETTLE_BAT main: DB connection failure, early exit)
CALC_FEE_ONLY (SETTLE_BAT main: zero-balance accounts jump to fee calculation)
SKIP_FEE (SETTLE_BAT main: fee-exempt accounts skip fee block)
RETRY_LOCK (SETTLE_BAT proc_settlement: retry SELECT FOR UPDATE on ORA-00054)
RPT_CLEANUP (RPT_DAILY main: final cleanup and disconnect)
global_state: g_log_fp, g_err_fp (file handles — may point to stdout/stderr on open failure)
g_err_msg[MAX_ERR_MSG] (reused error buffer across all contexts)
base_rate_tbl[10] (hardcoded interest rate table — duplicates DB data)
error_handling_patterns: GOTO-based recovery (main loop: ERR_HANDLE and SKIP_ACCT labels with ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT)
Silent ignore (some INSERT failures not checked)
Error count threshold (>1000 errors aborts batch)
WHENEVER SQLERROR GOTO (DB connect phase only)
business_rules: Interest: tiered by balance range (6 tiers: <1M, <10M, <50M, <100M, <500M, 500M+)
Interest: tiered by deposit period (4 tiers: <90d, <180d, <365d, 365d+ for fixed deposits)
Interest: VIP auto-premium +0.5% (cust_grade==1, no spc_rate override)
Interest: non-face-to-face account +0.2% (acct_type==7, applied in BOTH main and calc_interest)
Interest: COVID discount -0.1% for types 1,2 (2020-2023 period)
Interest: promotion rate from DB additive (TB_PROMO_RATE lookup)
Interest: daily = balance * (rate/100) / 365, rounded to won (ROUND_WON macro)
Interest: new_acct_type 41=+10%, 42=+20% multiplier for type 4
Interest: corporate checking (type 5) negative balance = +3% loan rate
Interest: duplicate inline+function calc with >1 won difference reconciliation
Tax: 15.4% of interest (income 14% + local 1.4%)
Tax: VIP fixed deposit exempt (acct_type==3, cust_grade==1)
Tax: product_cd starting with 'T' = preferential 9.5% rate
Fee: transaction-count-based per account type (500/300/0/400/200/100 won per extra txn)
Fee: balance-based daily (fee_rate% / 365)
Fee: maintenance monthly (1st of month only, exempt if balance >= 1M won)
Fee: grade-based free transaction count (VIP=unlimited, Gold+20, Silver+10, Bronze+5)
Fee: monthly cap 50,000 won
Fee: COVID exemption rate applied (from TB_COVID_EXEMPT)
Settlement: net = interest - tax - fee (negative means fee exceeds interest)
Commit: every N records (configurable via arg, default 100)
Error threshold: batch aborts after 1000 errors
Dormant: skip if last_txn_dt > 365 days ago (approximate date math)
Suspended (stat==2): fee exempt, interest only
Foreign currency (type 6): always skip
Zero balance + normal: fee only path (CALC_FEE_ONLY goto)

known_issues: Duplicate interest calculation: inline in main() AND via calc_interest() function, with >1 won reconciliation
Hardcoded DB credentials as fallback (BATCH_USER / batch1234 in plaintext)
Magic numbers throughout (account status 1/2/3/9, types 1-9, grade 1-5, tax 15.4%, etc.)
Date arithmetic is approximate (month=30 days, year=365 days, character arithmetic on date strings)
g_txn_cnt variable reused for batch control check AND transaction count (different semantic meanings)
g_txn_amt variable reused for per-transaction amount AND sum aggregate
COVID exemption applied in main() for fees AND 0.1% interest discount — commented-out duplicate in calc_fee()
Non-face-to-face +0.2% applied in both main() and calc_interest() (double application)
proc_settlement has commented-out balance UPDATE that previously caused double-posting
Race condition: MAX(SEQ)+1 for sequence generation instead of Oracle SEQUENCE
Inconsistent error handling: GOTO vs return codes vs silent ignore across functions
Dead code: 3 commented-out functions at end of SETTLE_BAT.pc with historical dates
Dead code: gen_csv_report stub in RPT_DAILY.pc (2021 attempt abandoned)
Mixed indentation: tabs in DECLARE SECTION, spaces elsewhere
Duplicate cursor declarations: c_acct and c_acct_br are nearly identical (only WHERE clause differs)
g_br_cd overwritten during fetch when mode is ALL (used for both filter and output)
